pages.full_text:
Malvern College Leavers Ball  Friday 8th July 2005  9pm – 1am

[glued-in printed card: aerial photograph of Malvern College buildings with numbered key]
[card text:]
1  Joffin's DJ & Bar
2  Jazz Trio, Bar & Chocolate Fountain  3  Cocktail Bar
4  Bouncy Castle  5  Pig Roast  6  Overnight Games Tent (from 12.30am)
You are kindly requested to keep your ticket with you during the evening.
photo_captions: Malvern College Leavers Ball  Friday 8th July 2005  9pm – 1am
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in printed event programme/ticket card for the Malvern College Leavers' Ball, featuring an aerial black-and-white photograph of the college buildings with numbered facilities labelled. Handwritten text below the card gives the event title and date/time. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
Friday July 8th 2005.
  Simon + John (17) + Eve set off at
4.25. for the Malvern Leavers' Ball. Two
days before, John heard he had got 36 in the
IB, + could go to Warwick. The following day
he was in London for the awful bombings.
  Sally + Minoru were at the ball, too,
Koroku dressed in his Kimono, which every-
one admired.
  Simon + Eve slept outside No 7, as
usual, + then walked over to the Grub for
breakfast, where Fran asked after Thomas.
  John + Charles' friend, Tom Paget,
should be walking to the van from the Abbey
Hotel for 8.30. Then we'll set off home,
+ on to Thorpeness in 2 cars, Thomas, Peter
Ben + Charles, too.
  Friday July 22nd
  John, Charles, Simon + Eve set off
at 5pm for France, waved off by Thomas
+ Peter. We are listening to the Ashes on the
pages.notes: The year 2005 is confirmed explicitly in the text ('Friday July 8th 2005'). Reference to 'the awful bombings' in London is consistent with the 7 July 2005 London bombings, the day before the ball. 'IB' likely refers to the International Baccalaureate. 'Koroku' may be a personal name; 'Minoru' and 'Sally' appear to be friends/family. The text cuts off at the bottom with 'the Ashes on the' — continues on the next page. No handwritten page number clearly visible in the top-right corner; a possible numeral appears but is ambiguous.
